name: Seafood Ravioli with Orange-Saffron Sauce
recipeIngredient: Zest and juice of two oranges
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean shrimp and reserve shells. Poach shrimp in well salted water with pickling spice. Discard the liquid.
2. Poach halibut in wine until set. Do not overcook.
3. Steam clams in white wine and garlic. Remove from shells and reserve liquid. Leave smaller clams whole and coarsely chop larger ones.
4. Gently toss seafood together and dress with olive oil. Set aside.
Sauce:
1. Steep saffron threads in white wine for 30 minutes.
2. Cook shrimp shells in hot oil just until they turn pink. Transfer to cheesecloth and set aside.
3. Sauté onion in the same oil used for shrimp shells until soft and golden, not brown. Add garlic and continue sautéing for an additional 5 minutes.
4. Deglaze the pan with white wine, poaching liquid, and clam broth. Add tomatoes and cheesecloth sachet.
5. Simmer for 1 3/4 hours. Add orange juice and zest and simmer for an additional 15 minutes.
Assembly:
1. Place about 1 tablespoon seafood mix on each large size ravioli pasta sheet.
2. Cover with the second layer, moisten borders with water, press out any air, and cut with a fluted ravioli cutter.
3. Lay on sheet pans lined with parchment paper and sprinkle with rice flour to keep dry. Cover with plastic.
Cooking:
1. Use a wide pan with plenty of water. Cook 3 or 4 raviolis at a time for 3 to 4 minutes, removing them with a slotted spoon.
2. Serve on a bed of the Spanish sauce.
